message:
ci: add frankenterm-core fuzz targets to CI smoke runs

Include fuzz_vt_parser and fuzz_grid_mutations in the CI fuzz job
alongside the existing ftui-core input parser targets. Each gets
a 20-second time-bounded smoke run.

Closes bd-lff4p.5.4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1321,7 +1321,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1","title":"FrankenTerm Core: grid + scrollback + VT/ANSI engine","description":"Scope
- Terminal data model (cells/lines/scrollback/selection) + parser/engine for VT/ANSI output streams.
- Designed so ftui can render directly (cell-buffer feed) and FrankenTerm can optionally accept ANSI streams for generality.

Deliverables
- New `frankenterm-core` crate: Grid, Scrollback, Selection, Cursor, Modes, Parser, Patch API.
- Unit + proptest coverage.
- Bench harnesses for parser throughput + grid ops.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:09.355447683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:09.355447683Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:09.355447683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm VT/ANSI support matrix + conformance fixtures","description":"Goal
- Define exactly what FrankenTerm will implement (and what it will intentionally NOT implement) for VT/ANSI output streams.

Deliverables
- Support matrix for: C0/C1 controls, CSI sequences, SGR, OSC (incl OSC 8), DEC private modes, cursor modes, scrolling, insert/delete, tabs.
- A conformance fixture corpus (byte streams + expected grid snapshots) with deterministic replay.
- Clear "compat" boundaries: which behaviors must match de-facto terminals.

Acceptance Criteria
- Parser work can be driven directly from this matrix and fixtures.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:49.449662450Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:49.449643043Z","close_reason":"Wrote comprehensive VT/ANSI support matrix at docs/spec/frankenterm-vt-support-matrix.md covering: C0/C1 controls, CSI (cursor, erase, insert/delete, scroll, modes), SGR (attributes, 16/256/RGB colors), OSC (title, hyperlinks, clipboard), DEC private modes, Kitty keyboard protocol, character sets. Includes 15 sections, explicit priority levels (Must/Should/Won't), parser state machine spec, conformance fixture format with 6 initial fixtures, and CI integration plan.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","title":"Bench: parser throughput + patch apply cost (core engine)","description":"Goal
- Build repeatable benchmarks for the core engine so optimization work is rigorous and gated.

Bench Corpus
- Maintain a realistic byte-stream corpus (build logs, dense SGR, markdown-ish output, unicode-heavy lines).
- Every benchmark run records corpus id/hash + git_sha so results are comparable.

Metrics
- Parser throughput (MB/s) for each corpus stream.
- Patch generation + patch apply cost under typical workloads.
- Memory footprint per 1k lines scrollback and under resize storms.

Outputs
- Machine-readable results (JSON) suitable for CI regression gates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Variance is characterized and documented; thresholds are set for meaningful regressions (p95 or distributional tests).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","title":"Implement: new crate frankenterm-core (API skeleton + workspace wiring)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/frankenterm-core` with the minimal public API surface and internal module layout.

Notes
- Keep dependencies minimal.
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core` passes (native).
- Basic types compile: Grid, Cell, Parser, Patch.
","notes":"Implemented crate skeleton in crates/frankenterm-core (Cell/SgrAttrs, Cursor+SavedCursor, Grid ops, Modes flags, Parser skeleton emitting Actions, Patch + Scrollback skeleton). Wired into workspace Cargo.toml. Verified: cargo check -p frankenterm-core, cargo test -p frankenterm-core.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanTower","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:05.512397161Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:05.512374489Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","title":"Implement: terminal grid data model (cells, lines, cursor, modes)","description":"Goal
- Implement the core in-memory model used by the terminal engine.

Requirements
- Cell model: text (grapheme), fg/bg, attrs (bold/italic/underline/etc), hyperlink id, and width.
- Grid model: fixed columns, rows; supports efficient row clears and spans.
- Cursor + modes: wrap, origin, insert, autowrap; plus placeholders for DEC modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests for all basic mutations.
- No panics on edge cases (min size, zero-length operations).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:58:22.262569874Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:58:22.262549566Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","title":"Implement: scrollback + resize/reflow policy","description":"Goal
- Support realistic scrollback and correct behavior under resize.

Requirements
- Scrollback buffer with configurable capacity.
- Clear reflow policy: what happens to wrapped lines on resize.
- Resize invariants: cursor position, viewport, selection (if any), and mode semantics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic replay fixtures for resize storms.
- Bench coverage for resize cost and memory.

@@ -1391,7 +1391,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.14","title":"Alien Artifact: exhaustive small-state model checking for terminal invariants","description":"Goal
- Go beyond randomized property tests: exhaustively explore small terminal states to prove invariants hold under all short sequences of operations.

Approach
- Enumerate state space for tiny grids (e.g. 2x2, 3x3) and bounded scrollback.
- Enumerate short operation sequences (parser ops, resize ops, selection ops) and verify invariants after each step.
- Use pruning + canonicalization to keep exploration tractable.

Deliverables
- A deterministic model-check runner producing a coverage report (state counts, transition counts, found counterexamples).
- Any counterexample becomes a regression fixture in the conformance corpus and/or trace corpus.

Acceptance Criteria
- Runs time-bounded in CI (small sizes) and extended locally (bigger bounds).
- Finds at least one non-trivial bug during development (expectation: state machines are hard).

- Turn benchmarks into regression gates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Baselines captured and checked in.
- CI fails on significant regressions (with documented thresholds).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.6","title":"Alien Artifact: formal cost models for caches + scheduling (renderer + parser)","description":"Goal
- Use principled math to design cache eviction, batching, and scheduling with explicit objective functions.

Examples
- Explicit loss functions for cache misses vs memory.
- Queueing/scheduling model for input vs render vs present.
- Evidence ledger: why a policy was chosen, with measured priors/posteriors.

Acceptance Criteria
- Documented models + experiments that justify the final policies.
- Results integrated into code comments and perf docs.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoRaven","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:43.079608273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:23:26.424835244Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T21:23:26.424816439Z","close_reason":"Completed: formal scheduler/cache objective functions + evidence docs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:43.079608273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:43.079608273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.7","title":"Optional: differential testing against reference terminal emulators","description":"Goal
- Validate FrankenTerm correctness via differential testing.

Approach
- Feed identical output byte streams to multiple reference emulators.
- Compare resulting grid snapshots and cursor state to FrankenTerm.

Acceptance Criteria
- Differential mismatches become tracked fixtures with root-cause notes.
","notes":"Progress (2026-02-08):
- Added differential harness: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/differential_terminal.rs comparing frankenterm-core parser-driven state against ftui_pty::virtual_terminal::VirtualTerminal (normalized grid text + cursor position).
- Added second independent reference harness: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/differential_extras_reference.rs comparing core against ftui_extras::terminal::TerminalState (driven by ftui_extras::terminal::AnsiParser).
- Added tracked known-mismatch ledger: tests/fixtures/vt-conformance/differential/known_mismatches.tsv (currently tracks csi_ich_insert_chars: ICH implemented by frankenterm-core but missing in the VirtualTerminal reference), plus a guard test ensuring known mismatches continue to mismatch.
- Documented both harnesses in docs/spec/frankenterm-correctness.md section 5.1.

Validation:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core (incl. differential harnesses + proptest invariants)
